name: kizano

metadata.name: kizano
metadata.summary: Library of functions that have served me no matter where I code.

documentation:
# libkizano.py
Kizano Library for the things I found very valuable in Python I can open-source.

Contains generic functions that pertain to no IP nor belong to any one organization or entity.

All code within this project is contained as-is without warranty or liability.
All code under this repository is relased to the public under the GNU License (GNU-GPLv3).

functions.name: lib.kizano.utils.literal_scalar
documentation: Maintains for us the discontinuity of a multiline scalar string.

functions.name: lib.kizano.utils.dump_yaml
documentation:
Same as write yaml, but returns the result instead.
Also use our representer to render multiline strings properly.

functions.name: lib.kizano.utils.read_yaml
documentation:
Read a file and parse it as yaml.
Return the data structure

functions.name: lib.kizano.utils.write_yaml
documentation:
Takes a data structure and writes it to file as yaml.
Except, don't use the default options provided by the package.
Use our custom decorators and whatnot to make it all purty <:

functions.name: lib.kizano.utils.dictmerge

classes.name: lib.kizano.Config

methods.name: lib.kizano.Config.getConfig
documentation: Static method for getting configuration for this app, be it cron, client or server.

Configuration object that tracks config in /etc/${APP_NAME}/config.yml and
in ~/.config/${APP_NAME}/config.yml

Returns a cached version of the config if we've asked the filesystem for it before for
faster IO in subsequent reads.

Reads system config in `/etc`, then overlays user config on top of that.
Arrays are appended when encountered.

functions.name: lib.kizano.logger.getLogger
lib.kizano.logger.syslogger
documentation:
Command line entry point that allows me to accept stdin as a lot of log messages
and then send them to the syslog endpoint.
My challenge with /usr/bin/logger is the time format is not iso8601 and I can't
control the format of the message.

methods.name: lib.kizano.logger.LocalSyslogHandler.emit
documentation:
Takes a log message and breaks it out into multiple messages if it exceeds 64k for syslogger.
Overload function since the original will throw an error if message is over the limit.
